title: trifolium angustifolium subsp. desvauxii
intro: Trifolium angustifolium subsp. desvauxii, also known as narrowleaf white clover, is a subspecies of the white clover plant, a type of legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The narrowleaf white clover has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Narrowleaf white clover can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
